title: False Positive Ratio (IDS CIM)
app: IDS + SOAR disposition fields mapped to CIM
spl:
index=security tag=ids disposition=*
| stats count(eval(disposition="false_positive")) as fp count as tot by signature
| eval fp_ratio=round(fp/tot*100,1)
| where fp_ratio>40
description: When analysts tag disposition, or vendor provides `action`/`priority`, measure noise ratio per `signature`.
value: When analysts tag disposition, or vendor provides `action`/`priority`, measure noise ratio per `signature`.
implementation: Requires workflow — without disposition, proxy with low-severity bulk.
cimSpl: | tstats `summariesonly` count from datamodel=Intrusion_Detection.IDS_Attacks where IDS_Attacks.severity IN ("low","informational") by IDS_Attacks.signature
knownFalsePositives: IDS and IPS noise from new signatures, internal vulnerability scans, and game-day simulations; use tuning and source IP context before high-severity pages.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: IDS + SOAR disposition fields mapped to CIM.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Optional `vendor_info` or custom field `disposition`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Requires workflow — without disposition, proxy with low-severity bulk.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=security tag=ids disposition=*
| stats count(eval(disposition="false_positive")) as fp count as tot by signature
| eval fp_ratio=round(fp/tot*100,1)
| where fp_ratio>40
```

Understanding this SPL

**False Positive Ratio (IDS CIM)** — When analysts tag disposition, or vendor provides `action`/`priority`, measure noise ratio per `signature`.

Documented **Data sources**: Optional `vendor_info` or custom field `disposition`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): IDS + SOAR disposition fields mapped to CIM. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: security.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=security, tags. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by signature** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **fp_ratio** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where fp_ratio>40` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count from datamodel=Intrusion_Detection.IDS_Attacks where IDS_Attacks.severity IN ("low","informational") by IDS_Attacks.signature
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**False Positive Ratio (IDS CIM)** — When analysts tag disposition, or vendor provides `action`/`priority`, measure noise ratio per `signature`.

Documented **Data sources**: Optional `vendor_info` or custom field `disposition`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): IDS + SOAR disposition fields mapped to CIM. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Intrusion_Detection.IDS_Attacks` — enable acceleration for that model.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Accelerate the CIM data model used in the optional tstats path, then run both the primary and `cimSpl` searches over a week that includes a known good baseline. Spot-check a few rows and tune thresholds; confirm no permission or tagging gap is hiding part of the population.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (noisy rules), Line chart (fp_ratio trend).
grandmaExplanation: We use the same kind of check described for "False Positive Ratio (IDS CIM)" once our data is shaped in a common, repeatable way across products. We help the team see trouble early, compare different tools fairly, and avoid building a one-off report for every vendor.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable Data Model Acceleration for the CIM `Intrusion_Detection.IDS_Attacks` dataset after normalizing this feed to the model; otherwise the `cimSpl` tstats search may return no rows.
